summary: A PhonePe commission calculator/tracking app that also includes a customer photos gallery feature where the user can save and view customer photos.

existing_features.title: Build a dashboard page displaying summary cards for: Total Transactions, Total Volume, Total Commission Earned, and Average Commission Rate. Include a data table listing all commission entries with sortable columns.

Provide a form to add a new commission entry with inputs for: merchant/agent name, category, transaction amount, commission rate (%), and transaction date. Auto-calculate commission earned from amount × rate.
Allow deleting a commission entry from the table with a confirmation prompt.

Apply a vibrant, modern visual theme inspired by PhonePe's brand identity using deep indigo/violet and bright magenta-pink accent colors, bold sans-serif typography, clean card-based layouts, and consistent use of color-coded commission indicators (green for high, amber for medium, red for low).
Add a PhonePe-style logo/icon for the app header.

Create a Customer Photos Gallery page/section in the frontend where users can view all saved customer photos in a responsive grid layout, upload new photos (with a name label) using a file input, and delete existing photos with a confirmation prompt. The gallery should be accessible via a navigation tab or button in the app header alongside the existing commission dashboard.

Add React Query hooks for the customer photos gallery: a query to fetch all customer photos and mutations to add and delete a customer photo, following the same pattern as the existing commission hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a backend data model to store commission entries with fields: transaction ID, transaction amount, commission rate (%), commission earned, transaction date, merchant/agent name, and category.
Implement backend query functions to calculate summary statistics: total transactions, total transaction volume, total commission earned, and average commission rate.
Add a customer photos gallery where users can save and view customer photos within the app.
Add a backend data model to store customer photos, with each entry containing a unique photo ID, a name/label, the image data (stored as Blob or Base64-encoded Text), and an upload timestamp.
Implement backend functions to upload a new customer photo (with name and image data), retrieve all saved customer photos, and delete a customer photo by ID.
